prompt: Name one commonly-praised idea, trend, or practice in modern knowledge work that you think is overrated, and explain why.
response: One commonly-praised idea in modern knowledge work that I believe is overrated is the emphasis on rapid iteration and "failing fast." While I agree that learning from mistakes and quickly pivoting based on feedback is important, an excessive focus on moving fast can lead to cutting corners, sacrificing quality, and adopting a superficial approach to problem-solving. The pursuit of speed should not come at the expense of thoroughness, deep thinking, and careful execution. Ultimately, sustainable success and meaningful innovation often require patience, diligence and taking the time to do things right.
